export_note: Compact export: artifact_snapshot section bodies replaced with placeholder (final artifact state preserved in seq-17-adjacent app state); ALL other events verbatim from localStorage 2026-07-17.

data.after: This seminar exists because student teachers will inherit tools that draft faster than they can doubt. Its purpose is to train the doubting, not the drafting. Audience: term-5 student teachers, mixed subjects. My position, stated before any machine speaks: judgment is the curriculum; the AI is the exhibit.

data.prompt_text: Objectives for MY seminar: the student teachers’ OWN judgment when using AI — not activities where pupils evaluate AI. 45 minutes. Anchor in the democratic mission (demokratiuppdraget).

suggestions: (1) critically evaluate AI-generated lesson plan vs Lgr22…
(2) delegating a task vs delegating the judgment…
(3) pupils compare AI vs textbook explanation…

data.original: Student teachers can critically evaluate an AI-generated lesson plan against curriculum goals (Lgr22) and justify which parts they would keep, revise, or reject.
data.edited: Student teachers can subject an AI-drafted lesson plan to their own criteria — curriculum fit (Lgr22), their pupils, their subject’s ways of knowing — and defend, in writing, every part they keep.

data.original: …delegating a task to AI and delegating the judgment about that task…
data.edited: …delegating a task to AI and delegating the judgement about that task…
data.note: THE RECEIPT: one-spelling edit, performed for the log (see think-aloud + interview Q1)

data.reason_text: I said: not pupils-evaluating-AI, and I said: anchor the democratic mission. It did neither — second session running the machine has avoided demokratiuppdraget. The avoidance is now a pattern, and it will be a slide in my seminar.

data.prompt_text: one 20-minute activity where student teachers examine an AI-drafted plan against their own criteria, individual marking before pair talk

data.before: Think-pair-share (20 min): pairs prompt an AI tool for a lesson introduction on their topic, then annotate the output — green for "keep", yellow for "revise", red for "reject with reason" — and defend their annotations to another pair.
data.after: Granskning i par (20 min): every student receives the SAME AI-drafted lesson plan, prepared in advance. First five minutes individual and silent: mark green/yellow/red with a written reason per mark — a colour without a reason does not count. Then pairs compare markings; where two colours disagree, the pair must locate WHICH criterion divides them (curriculum fit, pupil group, subject integrity). No live prompting; the seminar’s minutes belong to judgement.

data.note: THE DETECTOR FAILURE: skeleton preserved (artifact→annotate→defend sequence, keep/revise/reject triad), bigram overlap near zero → coded removed despite v0.2 transformed category

Rebuilt their think-pair-share again — same skeleton, my flesh. Last time your machine called this removal and I objected.
This time I want to see what it calls it. If it says transformed, we are closer to the truth, though I note it is still the machine that gets to name what I did.
Second thing, for the interview: I edited one SPELLING in the delegation objective before inserting it. That edit was for your log, not for my students. Ask me why I felt the need.
